title: Richly Roasted Uruguayan Pork Back Ribs Skewers
description: A show-off Uruguayan dish featuring pork back ribs with a fermented cheese-pull, served with fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, this recipe is kosher-friendly and perfect for special occasions. The combination of flavors and textures will leave your guests impressed.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together rice vinegar, olive oil, garlic, onion, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pork back ribs to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated pork back ribs onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and roast for 30 minutes.
While the pork is roasting, heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add a tablespoon of olive oil.
Add the cooked khao khuah rice to the skillet and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Remove the pork from the oven and brush with the fermented cheese-pull sauce.
Return the pork to the oven and roast for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until caramelized and crispy.
Serve the roasted pork back ribs skewers with the fry-grind-rice-khao-khua and garnish with chopped fresh herbs.
